name: Monad

x.formalRole: A monad is a monoid in the category of endofunctors: a type constructor M with a unit (return) and a multiplication (join/bind) that structure composition of context-carrying computations.
x.compositionRule: bind : M<A> → (A → M<B>) → M<B>. Kleisli arrows A→M<B> compose associatively, with return as the identity.
laws: Associativity:  (m >>= f) >>= g  ≡  m >>= (x → f(x) >>= g)
x.encoding: None of C/C++/C#/Java/Rust has higher-kinded types, so each monad (Option, Result, Future, list) implements flatMap / and_then concretely — there is no generic Monad interface spanning them.
x.useCases: Sequencing fallible (Result), optional (Option), asynchronous (Future/Task), stateful or logging computations without hand-threading the context.
misuseRisks: Monadic machinery over straight-line logic adds ceremony with no payoff
The “container/burrito” mental model misleads on effects like IO and State

def: An abstraction for sequencing context-dependent computations via bind (>>=) and a wrapping unit.
problem: Compose computations that carry context (optionality, failure, state, async, I/O) without manual plumbing.
mechanism: Provides `of`/`return` to lift a value and `bind`/`flatMap` to chain a value→M<value> function, threading context. Must satisfy left/right identity and associativity laws.
benefits: Separates effect plumbing from business logic
limits: No HKTs in C/C++/C#/Java/Rust → encodings are clumsy
whenNot: Teams without FP fluency under deadline
myths: "A monad is a container/burrito" — it is a composition interface; containers are one instance
